MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-28-2025 Vacated

MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT, ETC.
Off Calendar Sep-23-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Nerissa Pyett, Esq., LAW OFFICES OF OSTIN, KOTHARY & LOCK 619-744-6800 Attorney for Defendant/Cross-Complainant/Cross-Defendant, CANHUI HUANG
MICHELLE D. JEW, LAW OFFICES OF JOHNSON, ALVAREZ, ZAKARIAN, FERRARIS & CADENA (415) 713-9186
SanFranciscoLegal@allstate.com Attorneys for Defendants Alexander Smith and Sonia Yvette Solis, erroneoulsy sued and served as Sonia Yvettefor Defendant.
Jane Hong, CSR #11975, 909-831-2644 csrjane@hotmail.com

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ALEXANDER SMITH, SONIA SOLIS MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT, ETC.

Sonia Solis and Andrew Smith's motion "for determination of good faith settlement" is denied without prejudice. Solis drove the middle vehicle in a three-vehicle chain-reaction collision on Highway 101 in San Francisco. Plaintiff settled with Solis and Smith (apparently the vehicle's owner) for $30,000 insurance policy limits. But the court is provided insufficient information on several other key factors in a good faith settlement analysis: e.g., rough approximation of plaintiff's total recovery, settlor's proportionate liability, settlors' financial condition. (Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Jane Hong, CSR #11975, 909-831-2644 csrjane@hotmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)

Off Calendar Jan-17-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: DEFENDANT CANHUI HUANG's Motion To Compel Plaintiff Vernon Webbe'S Responses To Demand For Identification And Inspection Of Documents (Set One); Request For Sanctions.

Appearances (via Zoom):
Beth Hurley, Esq., of the LAW OFFICES OF OSTIN & KOTHARY, (619) 744-6800, for Defendant, CANHUI HUANG.
No other appearances.

Defendant stipulates to the matter being decided by the pro tem Judge. The pro tem Judge and Defendant (both appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

The matter is discussed. Upon conclusion of discussion, the court's tentative ruling is adopted as follows: No opposition filed. Moving party did not deliver hard copies of their papers to the Judge Pro Tem. Motion granted. Plaintiff to serve Code Compliant answers to Form Interrogatories Set One without objection on or before February 19, 2024 and Plaintiff and her counsel to pay $400 in sanctions on or before April 1, 2024 as defense counsel did not have to travel and appear at court in connection with the hearing on the Motion.

Counsel for prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order as to form and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at rkane1089@aol.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Robert Kane. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Counsel for prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order as to form and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at rkane1089@aol.com.
